attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who attacks from reduced groups at 12km to go, particularly effective on finishing climbs where he can absorb chasers in final 3km. Prefers to wait and counter rather than initiate early.
team_tactics: Operates independently in breakaways with minimal visible team support. Relies on positioning himself correctly within break dynamics rather than domestique-led setups. Others (e.g., VLASOV) use his wheels for pacing.
terrain_preference: Medium-mountain stages with final climb sprint finishes suit him best (won on Moosalp HC summit, Colle Brianza, uphill 300m sprint). Exposed on sustained high-tempo climbs where he visibly suffers before recovering.
vulnerability: Lacks pure finishing speed against top sprinters when fresher riders commit (EVENEPOEL, GROVES). Shows tactical impatience attacking solo <5km which fragments breakaway cooperation, allowing peloton to close. Cannot sustain GC-level chase tempo.
key_patterns: Elite recovery capacity: visibly suffering on penultimate climb, attacks successfully 12km later (multiple instances)
Breakaway specialist with tactical discipline: waits for opponents' premature moves before countering in final kilometers
Multiple Grand Tour stage wins from reduced breakaways, 2023 Giro double in three days
Photo-finish capable on HC summit finishes, breakthrough climbing sprint power since 2022
Fragile when forced to lead: solo moves <5km consistently backfire, needs others to pace first
form_trajectory: positive: Multiple 2023 Giro stage wins demonstrate peak form and refined tactical discipline compared to 2019 impatience. Breakthrough climbing power established 2022 continues.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 18
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 21
